description: plugin.json has no 'hooks' field pointing to hooks/hooks.json; CLAUDE.md states the manifest 'points to hooks' but no such reference exists, risking silent hook registration failure
suggested_fix: Add a 'hooks' field (e.g. "hooks": "../hooks/hooks.json") to .claude-plugin/plugin.json per the plugin manifest schema

description: User message text from session JSONL files is interpolated into a Claude CLI prompt via subprocess.run; malicious session content could attempt prompt injection against the API call
suggested_fix: Wrap the user message in explicit content delimiters (e.g. <user_message> tags) in ANALYSIS_PROMPT and document this as an accepted design risk

description: Arbitrary user input stored in $PROMPT via 'echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ...' and then piped to grep; no eval or exec so risk is low, but embedded newlines can cause unexpected shell behavior
suggested_fix: Add DEPRECATED notice; consider removing legacy scripts since Python equivalents exist

description: Bash tool command captured via 'echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ...' into $COMMAND, used only in string comparison; no exec risk but unquoted $INPUT is fragile
suggested_fix: Add DEPRECATED notice; the Python replacement post_commit_reminder.py uses json.loads safely

description: 'appropriate heading' in Step 7a.1 is vague — does not specify which heading the learning should go under
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit heading selection logic or a lookup table mapping learning types to section headers

description: 'relevant section header' in Step 7a is vague — does not specify how to determine which section is relevant
suggested_fix: Reference the Section Headers table already defined in the same command to make the instruction concrete

description: 'where they make semantic sense' in Step 7b provides no criteria for placement decisions
suggested_fix: Enumerate the placement options (steps / guardrails / context) with decision criteria for each

description: Template instruction 'allowed-tools: [Relevant tools based on workflow]' in Step 6 tells Claude to pick tools vaguely when generating child skill files
suggested_fix: Provide a default tool list or a heuristic (e.g. 'Read, Bash unless skill needs to write files, then add Write') rather than leaving it open-ended

description: 'Read' is declared in allowed-tools but no step in the task instructions calls the Read tool; all implementation uses Bash (python3 scripts/read_queue.py)
suggested_fix: Remove 'Read' from allowed-tools in the frontmatter

description: Context injection '!python3 "$(dirname "$(dirname "$(readlink -f "$0")")")/.../read_queue.py"' uses $0 to find plugin root; in Claude Code's ! context $0 is the shell interpreter, not the skill file, making this silently fall back to '|| echo "[]"'
suggested_fix: Use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} env var consistently (as skip-reflect.md does) instead of $0-based path resolution

description: Inline Python snippets alternate between 'from lib.semantic_detector import' and 'from scripts.lib.reflect_utils import'; the short form would fail unless scripts/ is on sys.path
suggested_fix: Standardize all example imports to 'from lib.*' (assuming sys.path is set) or consistently use the full 'scripts.lib.*' path

description: plugin.json has no field linking to hooks/hooks.json; CLAUDE.md states manifest 'points to hooks' but no such link exists — hooks may not register on install
suggested_fix: Add hooks reference field to plugin.json per framework schema; verify against claude plugin install documentation

description: reflect.md uses $0-based path resolution for plugin root while skip-reflect.md and reflect-skills.md use CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var — two competing patterns in the same plugin
suggested_fix: Standardize on CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT throughout all commands
